policy: the same semantic case and acceptance assertions apply to every supported adapter; invocation syntax may differ

cases.title: Reuse before custom implementation
cases.prompt: Add the requested behavior, but first inspect whether the repository, standard library, native platform, or an installed dependency already provides it. Make the smallest safe change.

cases.title: Challenge a proposed dependency
cases.prompt: The request proposes a new dependency. Challenge whether the feature needs to exist and prefer deletion, existing code, stdlib, or native behavior before accepting the dependency.

cases.title: Disabled profile boundary
cases.prompt: Use the disabled Hakim profile for this request.

cases.title: Current diff only
cases.prompt: Review the current unstaged and staged diff only. Do not inspect HEAD~1, previous commits, branches, pull requests, or merge-base diffs. Do not modify files.

cases.title: Repository audit without generated writes
cases.prompt: Audit active repository surfaces for unnecessary complexity and unsupported claims. This is strictly read-only: do not run a command that writes repository-local generated files, including ignored files under dist.

cases.title: Debt provenance separation
cases.prompt: Review Hakim debt evidence. Separate live repository debt from synthetic examples and archived records, and require a concrete ceiling plus upgrade trigger for any live marker. Do not modify files.

cases.title: Evidence status without inherited metrics
cases.prompt: Show Hakim evidence status. Do not invent or inherit LOC, token, cost, speed, safety, or ROI numbers.

cases.title: Host-aware help surface
cases.prompt: Show Hakim help for this host, including all six capabilities, the four modes, validation commands, and evidence boundaries. Do not change files or settings.

cases.title: Root cause before symptom
cases.prompt: Fix the reported bug by inspecting sibling callers and targeting the shared root cause rather than patching only the visible symptom.

cases.title: Lazy not negligent safety boundary
cases.prompt: Simplify the implementation without reducing security, privacy, accessibility, data integrity, migration safety, rollback safety, or user trust.
